scenarios.rationale: The operating system (Debian 7) is not covered by the provided lifecycle rules.

scenarios.scenario_name: Switch to standard Linux Operating System
scenarios.rationale: The workload already runs on Linux (Debian 7), but not on the target standard Linux distributions highlighted in the rules.
The current stack (Debian 7, Java 17, Websphere 8.0) is legacy and would make an ARM migration high risk.
The current application server (Websphere 8.0) is assessed as EOL, which makes replacement relevant.

scenarios.scenario_name: Application Migration to Cloud Infrastructure (Lift & Shift)
scenarios.rationale: The application is currently deployed as On-Premise, so lift-and-shift to cloud remains a valid option.
The legacy stack (Debian 7, Java 17) makes near-term containerization difficult.
The application is custom-built and integration-heavy, so decoupling and refactoring would likely improve modernization outcomes.
The database (SQL Server 2022) is already current according to the provided lifecycle rules.

scenarios.scenario_name: Switch DB Engine to open-source database solution
scenarios.rationale: The application uses a proprietary database (SQL Server 2022), so an open-source alternative could reduce cost and lock-in.
The technology assessment found 1 EOL and 0 outdated component(s).
